_meta.notes:
Synthesized PostToolUse:TaskUpdate stdin payload as it appears in an in-process subagent (teammate) frame: same session_id as the lead, but carries an `agent_id` field stamped by the Claude Code platform for the in-process Task-tool-spawned subagent. The agent_id field-presence IS the canonical actor-discriminator under Option 5 (the corrected hook predicate) — its presence distinguishes an in-process teammate fire from a lead-session fire that omits the field.

Fixture-source rationale (per plan v4 §Coverage Guards RECOMMENDED downgrade from HARD): synthesized fallback is acceptable because long-standing production consumers (dispatch_helpers.trustworthy_actor_name PreToolUse path + pact_context.resolve_agent_name PostToolUse path) read `agent_id` from PostToolUse stdin today and have been working in production for sessions; their working state empirically demonstrates the field IS present in in-process subagent frames and absent in lead-session fires. If `agent_id` were absent in production PostToolUse stdin, those consumers would be visibly broken across many surfaces. Captured-from-production fixture lands as the merge-precondition HARD GATE per plan §Coverage Guards #2 — post-merge fresh-session validation of secretary self-claim producing wake_inbox marker closes the empirical loop.

Paired with `task_update_production_shape.json` (lead-owned completed shape) for two-shape behavioral coverage of the lead-vs-teammate discriminator.

The `agent_id` value is a UUID-shape opaque identifier per the agent_id-field convention; not load-bearing on the predicate body (the predicate is field-PRESENCE not field-VALUE).
